If someone walks into your high-end furniture store off the street and they have gone out shopping on the weekend on their own, they may not have measured their spaces with a tape measure and brought their information because they said to themselves, we're just looking this weekend. So unless you exercised preparatory control and they were leads and they responded to the lead generation and they booked an appointment and one of the things that they were told to bring with them and you sent them a tape measure to do it with. So you can't preempt it with everybody but you could preempt it with some of it. So a lot of it is you sort of screwed up a lead in to the process one way or the other. Then sort of tactically to where he went, you need to have a really good urgency case by reason and by bribe, by incentive for moving quicker than you are. Then the prospect perhaps was initially prepared to do and it's best if you have the ability to drop down to a truly conditional sale. So I had a client for years selling vacation homes and sites on which you would build. And so the drop down to pay for a site right now was you really like this site because of the view of the lake and whatever, you can make a deposit for first right or refusal. And now for 90 days, if somebody else wants it, we're delaying them by a day, we're calling you and you will have first opportunity to try. So you can start to do these kind of conditional things. And the other thing is, which really takes it out of this program, but if you have much of this, you should have a really, really good follow up system for the non-buyer. Because there is gold there, they will come back, they can be brought back. And in many cases, it'll be over a period of time that will surprise you. Another tactical strategy is to find out, because you can often find out in a more elegant way than what you have to think about, is to use an NLP, what we call the agreement frame. So you can say, I understand that you need to think about it. Just to clarify my thinking, what specifically is it that you're going to think about? Because oftentimes they'll say, well, it's this and then that's the real objection. So you can actually get to the real objection as well. Let's see. Oh man. Okay. So we have a whole bunch more questions on objections. What we're going to do is we're going to handle it in our bonus training call. Sounds fair. The bonus training call has gone from like an hour to probably three days. Probably two hours, three hours. You didn't know that, but it's going to be good. So we have some questions. Why don't you read that one? Because that one's for me. Sure. I hope I can answer it. This is Molly for Dave. How to handle a situation when we've sold most people, but not the ultimate decision maker, and we can't get success or access? Excuse me. Well, you can get access. Right. That's the point. And you really haven't sold anybody. You've made a lot of good conversations. You haven't sold anybody. I mean, Jim Rohn tells a, I mean, I'm not trying to be a wise guy, but Jim Rohn tells a great story about how he went out and he did great presentations. And people would say, you're the best salesman I've ever seen. That was the best presentation. And he'd come back to the office and you'd say, I'm the greatest sales person. But wait a minute. How come I'm not making any sales? So I mean, it really comes to the fact of you've got to get to the decision maker. What we talked about earlier, if you're selling to the wrong people, you're not selling. If you let yourself be put in a position where the decision maker is not present, you're not selling. You're a professional visitor making friends. Right. You're passing out candy or cookies. So you went and you spent four hours in a boardroom with six people, but the guy who asked to make the decision wasn't there. You had a nice social activity and they like you. Relying on them to go make a sale for you is hopeless. And so it's very hard to get people to do this because they confuse activity with accomplishment and they can't bring themselves to refuse to come meet with somebody who is willing to meet with them. They let themselves be downgraded even at the last minute.
